data.title: Barclays stock advances after strong Q3 earnings; lifts NII guidance slightly
data.description:
The bank expects 2024 Group net interest income, excluding its investment bank and head office, of more than £11.0, compared with its prior guidance of ~£11.0B.
"We continue to exercise cost discipline and remain well capitalized with a Common Equity Tier1 (CET1) ratio at the end of the quarter of 13.8%," said CEO C. S. Venkatakrishnan.
Q3 profit before tax of £2.23B increased from £1.94B in Q2 and £1.89B in last year's Q3.
Credit impairment charges of £374M dipped from £384M in the previous quarter and £433M a year ago.
Q3 net interest income of £3.31B climbed from £3.06B in the prior quarter and £3.25B a year ago.
Total operating expenses of £3.96B eased from £4.01B in Q2 and edged up from £3.95B in Q3 2023.
Return on average tangible shareholders' equity of 12.3%, compared with its 2024 target of greater than 10%, improved from 9.9% in the previous quarter and 11.0% a year ago.
Loans and advances at amortized cost was £399.2M vs. £399.5M in Q2. Deposits at amortized cost slipped to £542.8M from $557.5M in the prior quarter.
Earlier, Barclays reports Q3 results

More on Barclays

  • Barclays PLC (BCS) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Barclays: Still Undervalued With Fundamental Resilience
  • Barclays: Heading In The Right Direction
  • UBS sees these 29 companies as industry leaders for 2030 for using tech to disrupt

data.title: WEX falls on disappointing quarter and guidance
data.description:
WEX (NYSE:WEX) shares fell sharply on Thursday after the payment processing provider disappointed investors with its weaker-than-anticipated guidance for the fourth quarter and full year 2024.

The Portland, Maine-based company’s shares were down 13% at $184.29 in morning trading.

WEX’s third quarter revenue increased 2% at $665 million, driven by growth in the company’s Benefits segment and Mobility business. However, both revenue and profit missed Wall Street estimates.

WEX said the shortfall in guidance was primarily due to the impact from macro trends including declining fuel prices and same-store sales, along with an isolated unplanned charge to finance fee revenue.

“We are reducing our outlook for the remainder of the year to reflect third quarter performance and our expectation that lower fuel prices and volume softness with Mobility customers will continue into the fourth quarter,” said WEX CFO Jagtar Narula.

The company expects fourth quarter revenue between $630 million and $640 million, while for the full year 2024, it expects revenue in the range of $2.62 billion to $2.63 billion. The consensus for Q4 and annual revenue stands at $$673.04 million and vs $2.69 billion, respectively.

Overall, the stock lost over 5% so far this year, compared to the nearly 22% rise in the broader S&P500 Index.

data.title: First Citizens stock tanks after 2024 guidance revised down, Q3 earnings miss
data.description:
First Citizens BancShares (NASDAQ:FCNCA) stock sank 9% in Thursday morning trading after revising down its 2024 guidance for net interest income and loans and deposits, in addition to delivering Q3 earnings that fell short of Wall Street expectations.

The lender, which last year acquired failed Silicon Valley Bank, now expects full-year 2024 net interest income of $7.08B-$7.18B, compared with the prior guidance of $7.2B-$7.3B and the $7.21B Visible Alpha consensus.

Adjusted noninterest income for the year is expected to be $1.89B-$1.91B, vs. $1.85B-$1.90B in the previous view, and adjusted noninterest expense is targeted to be $4.78B-$4.79B, vs. $4.65B-$4.70B in the prior range, according to a slide presentation.

It now sees FY24 loans and leases of $138B-$140B, down from the prior guidance of $143B-$146B, and deposits of $150B-$153B, down from the prior goal of $153B-$155B.

Q3 adjusted EPS of $45.87, missing the $47.32 average analyst estimate, fell from $50.87 in Q2 and $55.92 in Q3 2023.

Net interest income slipped to $1.80B in Q3 from $1.82B in Q2 and $1.99B in Q3 of last year. NII is expected to retreat further to $1.65B-$1.75B in Q4, the earnings slides showed. Net interest margin of 3.53% vs. 3.64% in Q2 and 4.07% in the year-earlier period.

Adjusted noninterest income fell to $474M from $479M in Q2 and rose from $468M a year before. Q4's non-GAAP noninterest income is seen staying in the $460M-$480M range.

Provision for credit losses was $117M, up from $95M in Q2 and down from $192M in Q3 2023.

Total deposits edged up to $151.6B at Sept. 30 from $151.1B at June 30. Loans and leases of $138.7B at the end of Q3 compared with $139.3B at the end of Q2. Q4 loans/leases and deposits are expected to be $138B-$140B and $150-$153B, respectively.

data.description:
